paper.title: Design-time business process compliance assessment based on multi-granularity semantic information
paper.affiliation: School of Computer Science and Technology, Hangzhou Dianzi University, Hangzhou, China

pipeline_stages.name: Modality Classification
pipeline_stages.method: BERT pre-training (general + legal corpora) + fine-tuning on labeled regulatory sentences

pipeline_stages.name: Semantic Information Extraction
pipeline_stages.method: Constituent parse trees + marker-based rule templates
pipeline_stages.output: 6 concepts: modality, actor, action, condition, constraint, exception

pipeline_stages.name: Rule Base Construction
pipeline_stages.method: Match extracted concepts to structured rule records

pipeline_stages.name: Compliance Checking
pipeline_stages.method: Text similarity matching (τ threshold) + violation detection
pipeline_stages.output: 3 violation types: missing action, incorrect actor, out-of-order execution

concepts.modality: The auxiliary representing the action's modality
concepts.actor: The role responsible for the execution of an activity
concepts.condition: The conditions stating when the regulation applies
concepts.constraint: The restriction placed on the applicability of a legal provision
concepts.exception: The possible exceptions indicating when the provision does not apply

datasets.name: Austrian Income Tax Code — Modality Classification
12 BPMN Process Models — Matching Evaluation
datasets.domain: Austrian energy supplier — smart meter scenarios
datasets.rule_base: Parsed from regulatory documents related to smart meters
datasets.validation: Built and validated by domain experts

semantic_extraction.f1_derivation_note: F1 computed as 2*(P*R)/(P+R) from reported precision (0.979) and recall (0.953), not directly reported in Table 8

matching_evaluation.notes: 0.889 is a single-process-model AP (not overall MAP). 0.801 is the Overall(MAP) value directly reported in Sun Table 9 at τ=0.8. Do not recompute it from the visible per-model AP list — the per-model AP list may be incomplete/partially captured in extracted evidence and must not be used to derive the overall MAP.

ablation_findings: Legal corpus pre-training outperforms general corpus (bert-legal-uncased > bert-base-uncased)
Model size increase (base→large) does not significantly improve classification
Cased vs uncased: uncased slightly better for legal text
Winter et al. (2020) has higher recall but much lower precision than Sun's method
Marker-based rules can be extended from initial sets
Best matching threshold τ=0.8 for both Austrian energy (overall MAP=0.801) and GDPR (overall MAP=0.840) scenarios. Note: 0.889 is a single-model AP, not overall MAP.

data_availability.statement: The datasets generated during and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
